title: Hornet Removal in Flushing, Queens | $350-$550

intro: Hornets pose a serious threat to Flushing residents and businesses, with their aggressive behavior and painful stings creating dangerous situations around homes, gardens, and commercial properties. Best Pest Control NYC provides professional hornet removal services throughout Flushing, from the bustling streets near Main Street to the quiet residential neighborhoods surrounding Kissena Park. Our experienced technicians safely eliminate hornet nests and implement preventative measures to protect your property year-round.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Flushing's diverse community for years, understanding the unique pest challenges faced by both historic single-family homes and modern high-rise buildings in this vibrant Queens neighborhood. Our licensed technicians use safe, effective methods to remove hornet nests quickly, with transparent pricing from $350-$550 and same-day emergency service available throughout Flushing and surrounding areas.
localContext: Flushing's mix of lush green spaces like Flushing Meadows-Corona Park, dense residential areas, and thriving commercial districts along Northern Boulevard creates ideal conditions for hornet nesting, especially during warmer months. From Tudor-style homes in historic districts to modern condominiums near the LIRR station, our team has experience safely removing hornets from every type of Flushing property.

faqs.question: How much does hornet removal cost in Flushing, Queens?
faqs.answer: Hornet removal services in Flushing typically range from $350 to $550, depending on nest location, size, and accessibility. Ground-level nests are generally less expensive, while nests in high locations like roof eaves or tall trees may cost more due to additional equipment and safety measures required. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing before any work begins.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a hornet nest in Flushing?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and emergency hornet removal services throughout Flushing. Most hornet nest removals are completed within 1-2 hours, including inspection, treatment, and nest removal. During peak season (late summer and early fall), we prioritize emergency calls involving nests near entryways, playgrounds, or high-traffic areas to ensure the safety of your family or customers.

faqs.question: What's the difference between hornets, wasps, and yellow jackets?
faqs.answer: Hornets are larger than typical wasps and yellow jackets, with bald-faced hornets (common in Flushing) reaching up to 3/4 inch in length with distinctive white and black markings. Hornets build large, enclosed paper nests usually in trees or under eaves, while yellow jackets often nest in ground cavities or wall voids. Hornets are generally more aggressive when defending their nests and can sting multiple times, making professional removal essential.

faqs.question: Are hornet removal treatments safe for my family and pets in Flushing?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and follow strict safety protocols to protect your family, pets, and the environment. Our technicians assess each situation individually and apply targeted treatments directly to the nest, minimizing exposure to surrounding areas. We provide clear instructions on any temporary precautions needed, typically recommending keeping children and pets indoors for 2-4 hours during and after treatment.
